Title: Three AtCesA6-like members enhance biomass production by distinctively promoting cell growth in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: primary cell wall cellulose synthesis; CesA6-like module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: polymerization step in cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils in the primary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; supported as an important control component because overexpression increased cellulose production and CSC motility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major structural cell wall biopolymer contributing to cell expansion, mechanical strength, and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primary and secondary cell walls; hypocotyls, roots, inflorescence stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in primary wall-forming seedling tissues; D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots among examined tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpressed in transgenic seedlings and mature plants; assessed in D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes cellulose accumulation, seedling growth, and biomass yield when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; Western blot; qPCR; crystalline cellulose quantification; crossing with proAtCesA3::GFP-AtCesA3 to assess CSC movement; growth and cell wall phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtCesA2 increased hypocotyl/root growth relative to WT. 2. It elevated expression of other major primary wall CesAs including AtCesA5, AtCesA6, AtCesA1 and often AtCesA3. 3. It increased GFP-CesA3 particle velocity, crystalline cellulose content, and contributed to higher mature-plant biomass and thicker secondary walls.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCesA1; AtCesA3; AtCesA5; AtCesA6; GFP-AtCesA3; CesA4; CesA7; CesA8
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-grown seedlings; light-grown seedlings; soil-grown 7-week mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of D6 dark-grown seedlings (accession NA); qPCR; metabolite/cell wall composition analyses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported for AtCesA2 overexpression; altered wall composition included relatively lower hemicellulose proportion and monosaccharide shifts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for biomass engineering to increase cellulose and total biomass; may be stacked with other favorable primary wall CesAs, but effects on wall composition should be monitored
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtCesA2 belongs to the AtCesA6-like subgroup; unlike AtCesA3, AtCesA9, or AtCesA7 overexpression, AtCesA2 overexpression improved growth and cellulose deposition

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: primary cell wall cellulose synthesis; CesA6-like module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: polymerization step in cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils in the primary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; supported as an important control component because overexpression increased cellulose production and CSC motility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major structural cell wall biopolymer contributing to cell expansion, mechanical strength, and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primary and secondary cell walls; hypocotyls, roots, inflorescence stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in primary wall-forming seedling tissues; D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots among examined tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpressed in transgenic seedlings and mature plants; assessed in D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes cellulose accumulation, seedling growth, and biomass yield when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; Western blot; qPCR; crystalline cellulose quantification; crossing with proAtCesA3::GFP-AtCesA3 to assess CSC movement; growth and cell wall phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtCesA5 increased hypocotyl/root growth relative to WT. 2. It elevated expression of other primary wall CesAs including AtCesA2, AtCesA6, AtCesA1 and often AtCesA3. 3. It increased cellulose accumulation and enhanced mature-plant biomass and secondary wall deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCesA1; AtCesA3; AtCesA2; AtCesA6; GFP-AtCesA3; CesA4; CesA7; CesA8
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-grown seedlings; light-grown seedlings; soil-grown 7-week mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of D6 dark-grown seedlings (accession NA); qPCR; metabolite/cell wall composition analyses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported for AtCesA5 overexpression; altered wall composition included relatively lower hemicellulose proportion and monosaccharide shifts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising engineering target for higher cellulose and biomass; can be considered with other CesA6-like members for cell wall enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtCesA5 also has reported roles in seed mucilage secondary wall cellulose production, but this paper focuses on biomass/cell wall effects in whole plants

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: primary cell wall cellulose synthesis; CesA6-like module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: polymerization step in cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils in the primary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; strongest evidence among tested genes for a key control role in enhancing cellulose synthesis and wall mechanics when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major structural cell wall biopolymer contributing to cell expansion, mechanical strength, and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primary and secondary cell walls; hypocotyls, roots, inflorescence stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in primary wall-forming seedling tissues; D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots among examined tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpressed in transgenic seedlings and mature plants; assessed in D9 hypocotyls and L9 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes cellulose accumulation, cell elongation/division, secondary wall thickening, mechanical strength, and biomass yield when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; Western blot; qPCR; crystalline cellulose quantification; AFM macrofibril imaging; crossing with proAtCesA3::GFP-AtCesA3 for CSC motility; TEM of wall thickness; AFM Young's modulus; growth phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtCesA6 enhanced hypocotyl and root growth. 2. It increased expression of other primary wall CesAs and accelerated GFP-CesA3 particle movement at the plasma membrane. 3. It increased crystalline cellulose in seedlings and stems, enlarged cellulose macrofibrils, thickened primary and secondary walls, improved mechanical strength, and raised biomass yield.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCesA1; AtCesA3; AtCesA2; AtCesA5; GFP-AtCesA3; CesA4; CesA7; CesA8
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-grown seedlings; light-grown seedlings; soil-grown 7-week mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of D6 dark-grown seedlings (accession NA); qPCR; metabolite/cell wall composition analyses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; overexpression altered wall composition and macrofibril organization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering target to raise cellulose-rich biomass and stem mechanical strength in crops; deployment should monitor cell wall composition and integrity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study provides the strongest mechanistic support for AtCesA6 among tested genes, including CSC velocity, macrofibril size, and wall mechanics
